  "text": "The HTTP filter chain extension mechanism in Envoy is composed of several key components working together:

## Core Filter Interfaces (envoy/http/filter.h)
The foundation consists of filter interface classes:
- `StreamFilterBase` - Base interface for all filters with lifecycle methods like onDestroy() and onStreamComplete()
- `StreamDecoderFilter` - Interface for filters processing request data (headers, data, trailers) with methods like decodeHeaders(), decodeData(), decodeTrailers()
- `StreamEncoderFilter` - Interface for filters processing response data (headers, data, trailers) with methods like encodeHeaders(), encodeData(), encodeTrailers()
- `StreamFilter` - Combines both decoder and encoder interfaces
- Return status enums: `FilterHeadersStatus`, `FilterDataStatus`, `FilterTrailersStatus` control filter chain iteration

## Filter Chain Manager and Factory Interfaces (envoy/http/filter_factory.h)
These interfaces define the contract for filter chain creation:
- `FilterChainFactory` - Called when a new HTTP stream is created to instantiate the filter chain
- `FilterChainManager` - Acts as the "sink" that receives filter factories and builds the filter chain
- `FilterChainFactoryCallbacks` - Provides methods like addStreamDecoderFilter(), addStreamEncoderFilter(), addAccessLogHandler() to register filters with the chain
- `FilterFactoryCb` - Typedef for the factory callback function that creates and registers filter instances
- `FilterContext` - Contextual information about the filter (config name, filter name)
- `FilterChainOptions` - Options for customizing filter chain creation

## Filter Factory Registry (envoy/server/filter_config.h)
The registration system:
- `NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory` - Base interface for HTTP filter factories registered by name
- `UpstreamHttpFilterConfigFactory` - Base interface for upstream HTTP filters
- These implement the Registry pattern allowing filter configurations to be looked up and instantiated

## Per-Request Filter Chain Execution (source/common/http/filter_manager.h, filter_manager.cc)
The core execution engine:
- `FilterManager` - Manages execution of the filter chain for a single request/response stream, implementing both decoder and encoder phases
- `DownstreamFilterManager` - Specialized FilterManager for downstream requests
- `ActiveStreamFilterBase` - Base for active filter wrappers that handle filter callbacks and state transitions
- `ActiveStreamDecoderFilter` - Wraps a decoder filter and manages its execution during decode phase
- `ActiveStreamEncoderFilter` - Wraps an encoder filter and manages its execution during encode phase
- The FilterManager iterates through decoder filters in sequence during request processing and encoder filters in reverse sequence during response processing
- Key methods: `decodeHeaders()`, `encodeHeaders()`, `decodeData()`, `encodeData()`, `decodeTrailers()`, `encodeTrailers()`

## HTTP Connection Manager (source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.h, conn_manager_impl.cc)
The main HTTP protocol handler:
- `ConnectionManagerImpl` - Network filter that manages HTTP connections and creates per-stream FilterManagers
- Creates a new FilterManager for each HTTP stream
- Calls the FilterChainFactory when a new stream arrives to build the filter chain

## Filter Chain Creation Flow (source/common/http/filter_chain_helper.h, filter_chain_helper.cc, source/extensions/filters/network/http_connection_manager/config.h, config.cc)
The process of instantiating filter chains:
- `HttpConnectionManagerConfig` - Configuration object that implements FilterChainFactory interface
- `FilterChainUtility::createFilterChainForFactories()` - Static utility that applies filter factories to the FilterChainManager
- `FilterChainHelper` - Template class that manages the complete filter chain creation process including dynamic filter loading
- When a new stream arrives, HttpConnectionManagerConfig::createFilterChain() is called
- This calls FilterChainUtility::createFilterChainForFactories() which applies each configured filter factory in order
- Each factory callback is applied via FilterChainManager::applyFilterFactoryCb()

## Filter Factory Base Classes (source/extensions/filters/http/common/factory_base.h)
Reusable implementations for filter factories:
- `FactoryBase<ConfigProto, RouteSpecificConfigProto>` - Template base for downstream-only filters
- `DualFactoryBase<ConfigProto, RouteSpecificConfigProto>` - Template base for filters supporting both upstream and downstream contexts
- Provides default implementations of createFilterFactoryFromProto() methods

## Dynamic Filter Configuration (source/common/filter/config_discovery_impl.h)
Support for dynamic filter configuration:
- `HttpDynamicFilterConfigProviderImpl` - Allows filter configuration to be updated at runtime via xDS without restarting
- Manages subscription to filter config updates

## Helper Utilities (source/extensions/filters/http/common/pass_through_filter.h)
- `PassThroughFilter` - Convenient base class for filters that don't modify the stream data
- Provides default implementations of all filter methods that just continue the chain

This architecture enables Envoy's flexible HTTP filter extensibility where filters can be plugged in at configuration time, dynamically loaded via xDS, and executed in sequence with control over iteration flow based on status returns."
}

**Target Repositories (version-pinned mirrors):**

- `github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e$` filter

Scope ALL keyword_search/nls_search queries to these repos.
Use the repo name as the `repo` parameter for read_file/go_to_definition/find_references.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

# Envoy HTTP Filter Chain Extension Architecture

## Your Task

Find all C++ source files in envoyproxy/envoy that define the HTTP filter chain extension mechanism: the filter factory registry, filter context, and the per-request filter chain execution.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the domain domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2, sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367, sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6, sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2` (envoyproxy/envoy)
- `sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367` (envoyproxy/data-plane-api)
- `sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6` (envoyproxy/go-control-plane)
- `sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e` (grpc/grpc)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],

  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

## Summary

The answer includes **14 key C++ files**, **23 major symbols**, and **7 key execution functions** organized into logical sections:

### Core Components:

1. **Filter Interfaces** - Define what filters can do (StreamDecoderFilter, StreamEncoderFilter, StreamFilter)

2. **Filter Factory Interfaces** - Define how filter chains are created (FilterChainFactory, FilterChainManager, FilterChainFactoryCallbacks)

3. **Filter Registry** - Enables plugin registration (NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory, UpstreamHttpFilterConfigFactory)

4. **Per-Request Execution Engine** - Handles filter chain execution for each request (FilterManager, ActiveStreamFilterBase, ActiveStreamDecoderFilter, ActiveStreamEncoderFilter)

5. **HTTP Connection Manager** - Integrates with the HTTP protocol handler (ConnectionManagerImpl)

6. **Filter Chain Creation** - Orchestrates filter chain instantiation (HttpConnectionManagerConfig, FilterChainUtility)

7. **Base Classes** - Provides reusable implementations (FactoryBase, DualFactoryBase)

The document provides a detailed narrative explaining how these components work together to enable Envoy's flexible HTTP filter extensibility, including support for dynamic filter loading via xDS and sequential filter execution with control flow based on status returns.

The answer has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json`.

  "text": "The HTTP filter chain extension mechanism in Envoy is composed of several key components working together:

## Core Filter Interfaces (envoy/http/filter.h)
The foundation consists of filter interface classes:
- `StreamFilterBase` - Base interface for all filters with lifecycle methods like onDestroy() and onStreamComplete()
- `StreamDecoderFilter` - Interface for filters processing request data (headers, data, trailers) with methods like decodeHeaders(), decodeData(), decodeTrailers()
- `StreamEncoderFilter` - Interface for filters processing response data (headers, data, trailers) with methods like encodeHeaders(), encodeData(), encodeTrailers()
- `StreamFilter` - Combines both decoder and encoder interfaces
- Return status enums: `FilterHeadersStatus`, `FilterDataStatus`, `FilterTrailersStatus` control filter chain iteration

## Filter Chain Manager and Factory Interfaces (envoy/http/filter_factory.h)
These interfaces define the contract for filter chain creation:
- `FilterChainFactory` - Called when a new HTTP stream is created to instantiate the filter chain
- `FilterChainManager` - Acts as the "sink" that receives filter factories and builds the filter chain
- `FilterChainFactoryCallbacks` - Provides methods like addStreamDecoderFilter(), addStreamEncoderFilter(), addAccessLogHandler() to register filters with the chain
- `FilterFactoryCb` - Typedef for the factory callback function that creates and registers filter instances
- `FilterContext` - Contextual information about the filter (config name, filter name)
- `FilterChainOptions` - Options for customizing filter chain creation

## Filter Factory Registry (envoy/server/filter_config.h)
The registration system:
- `NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory` - Base interface for HTTP filter factories registered by name
- `UpstreamHttpFilterConfigFactory` - Base interface for upstream HTTP filters
- These implement the Registry pattern allowing filter configurations to be looked up and instantiated

## Per-Request Filter Chain Execution (source/common/http/filter_manager.h, filter_manager.cc)
The core execution engine:
- `FilterManager` - Manages execution of the filter chain for a single request/response stream, implementing both decoder and encoder phases
- `DownstreamFilterManager` - Specialized FilterManager for downstream requests
- `ActiveStreamFilterBase` - Base for active filter wrappers that handle filter callbacks and state transitions
- `ActiveStreamDecoderFilter` - Wraps a decoder filter and manages its execution during decode phase
- `ActiveStreamEncoderFilter` - Wraps an encoder filter and manages its execution during encode phase
- The FilterManager iterates through decoder filters in sequence during request processing and encoder filters in reverse sequence during response processing
- Key methods: `decodeHeaders()`, `encodeHeaders()`, `decodeData()`, `encodeData()`, `decodeTrailers()`, `encodeTrailers()`

## HTTP Connection Manager (source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.h, conn_manager_impl.cc)
The main HTTP protocol handler:
- `ConnectionManagerImpl` - Network filter that manages HTTP connections and creates per-stream FilterManagers
- Creates a new FilterManager for each HTTP stream
- Calls the FilterChainFactory when a new stream arrives to build the filter chain

## Filter Chain Creation Flow (source/common/http/filter_chain_helper.h, filter_chain_helper.cc, source/extensions/filters/network/http_connection_manager/config.h, config.cc)
The process of instantiating filter chains:
- `HttpConnectionManagerConfig` - Configuration object that implements FilterChainFactory interface
- `FilterChainUtility::createFilterChainForFactories()` - Static utility that applies filter factories to the FilterChainManager
- `FilterChainHelper` - Template class that manages the complete filter chain creation process including dynamic filter loading
- When a new stream arrives, HttpConnectionManagerConfig::createFilterChain() is called
- This calls FilterChainUtility::createFilterChainForFactories() which applies each configured filter factory in order
- Each factory callback is applied via FilterChainManager::applyFilterFactoryCb()

## Filter Factory Base Classes (source/extensions/filters/http/common/factory_base.h)
Reusable implementations for filter factories:
- `FactoryBase<ConfigProto, RouteSpecificConfigProto>` - Template base for downstream-only filters
- `DualFactoryBase<ConfigProto, RouteSpecificConfigProto>` - Template base for filters supporting both upstream and downstream contexts
- Provides default implementations of createFilterFactoryFromProto() methods

## Dynamic Filter Configuration (source/common/filter/config_discovery_impl.h)
Support for dynamic filter configuration:
- `HttpDynamicFilterConfigProviderImpl` - Allows filter configuration to be updated at runtime via xDS without restarting
- Manages subscription to filter config updates

## Helper Utilities (source/extensions/filters/http/common/pass_through_filter.h)
- `PassThroughFilter` - Convenient base class for filters that don't modify the stream data
- Provides default implementations of all filter methods that just continue the chain

This architecture enables Envoy's flexible HTTP filter extensibility where filters can be plugged in at configuration time, dynamically loaded via xDS, and executed in sequence with control over iteration flow based on status returns."
}
